entry.title: Re: Automatic sales order creation
entry.content:
Thanks for the follow‑up in the comment, @35400__.

✅ Short answer

  • 🇧🇷 Brazil: There is no indication that the service contract / periodic billing functionality is restricted for Brazil — it is part of standard service contract billing capabilities.
  • 🔧 Service contract type: Yes, a service contract document type must be available in your scope; it is not the same as sales contracts (quantity/value).
  • ⚙️ Periodic billing plan: It is set directly on the service contract item, but it depends on configuration (billing plan rules and item/category settings).

✅ 1) Is this supported for Brazil?

From available SAP Help documentation, the service contract + periodic billing plan scenario is part of the standard global functionality and not described as country-restricted.
  • Periodic billing plans are a core mechanism of service contracts:
    “You use periodic billing plans to schedule individual dates for the billing of service contracts…” [help.sap.com]
👉 There is no explicit country limitation mentioned in the SAP Help sources reviewed.
✅ Conclusion:
  • Supported in general
  • But always confirm:
    • country-specific e-invoicing requirements (Brazil)
    • scope items like Document and Reporting Compliance if needed

✅ 2) Why you only see quantity/value contracts

What you're currently seeing in Manage Sales Contracts are SD (sales) contracts, not service contracts.
👉 Important distinction:
Type App PurposeService Contract
Sales Contract (quantity/value)Manage Sales ContractsFramework for goods → requires release orders
Manage Service ContractsRecurring services + periodic billing
✅ This matches SAP behavior:
  • Sales contracts require release (sales order) to fulfill [help.sap.com]
  • Service contracts directly support billing via billing plans

✅ 3) How to “activate” service contracts

⚠️ This is where things depend on scope activation (CBC) — not just configuration.

Typically required:

You need to ensure that Service Management scope is activated, which includes:
  • Service contracts
  • Service orders
  • Related billing functionality
👉 In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition this is controlled by:
  • Scope items enabled in CBC (Central Business Configuration)
  • Not by simply creating a new document type
⚠️ Important nuance (validated best practice):
  • New scope cannot always be added after initial scoping without system reprovisioning in some cases 
👉 So the steps are typically:
  1. Check CBC → “Define Scope”
  2. Verify service-related scope items are active
  3. Ensure business catalogs/roles allow access to:
    • Manage Service Contracts app
✅ If the app is missing, it usually means:
  • Scope not activated
  • Or role/catalog missing

✅ 4) Periodic billing plan setup — where it happens

✔ No separate document-type configuration required (conceptually)

  • The billing plan is defined at the service contract item level
From SAP Help:
“Billing plans in the contract items control periodic billing…” [help.sap.com]

✔ However, configuration is still required

You need:
  • Billing plan types
  • Date rules
  • Settlement rules
These are configured centrally:
“The system uses various rules to generate periodic billing plans… based on configuration rules and date management.” [help.sap.com]

✔ How it works functionally

  1. Create service contract
  2. Maintain service contract item
  3. Assign billing plan (monthly, etc.)
  4. System generates billing request lines automatically

✅ Key takeaways

  • ✅ You are currently looking at the wrong contract type (SD vs Service)
  • ✅ Service contracts must be activated via scope, not just created
  • ✅ Periodic billing is native to service contract items
  • ✅ No standard automated sales order creation — invoices come directly from the contract

✅ Follow‑up questions (important to confirm)

To guide you precisely, it would help to know:
  • Do you already have access to the “Manage Service Contracts” app?
  • Which scope items were activated in CBC (especially Service Management)?
  • Are you planning pure recurring billing or also logistics/service execution?

✅ AI transparency note

This response was prepared with AI‑powered assistance and validated against official SAP Help Portal documentation. Please always verify against your customer’s system configuration and release.
 
If this answer helped you, please mark the response accordingly. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Sales Performance Plan vs Actual
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, adding a custom field in the Sales Performance – Plan/Actual app does not automatically allow you to define a formula or calculation for that field. Custom fields are mainly used as dimensions/attributes for analysis, not as calculated KPIs. [community.sap.com], [help.sap.com]

If your requirement is:

Percentage Achievement = Actual Quantity / Planned Quantity × 100

then you generally have these options:

Option 1: Create a Custom Analytical Query (Recommended)

SAP provides released CDS views for Sales Performance data:

  • I_SlsPerformancePlanActualCube
  • C_SlsPerfPlanActualQuery

These can be used in:

  • Custom CDS Views
  • Custom Analytical Queries
  • KPIs and Reports
  • SAC Stories

You can create a calculated measure in a Custom Analytical Query:

 

Achievement % = (Actual Quantity / Planned Quantity) * 100

 

and then expose it in your report or KPI. [community.sap.com]

Option 2: Create a Custom CDS View

Build a custom CDS view on top of I_SlsPerformancePlanActualCube and define a calculated field/measure there. Then consume that CDS in an analytical query or report. [community.sap.com]

What you cannot do

If you create a field in Custom Fields and Logic and enable it for:

  • Manage Sales Plans
  • Sales Performance – Plan/Actual

that field is primarily available as a dimension for filtering, grouping, or drill-down. There is no place in the Custom Fields and Logic app where you can enter a formula like:

 

Actual Qty / Planned Qty * 100

 

for the standard Sales Performance app. [community.sap.com]

My recommendation

For a percentage KPI in Sales Performance – Plan/Actual, use:

  1. Custom Analytical Queries
  2. Data source: I_SlsPerformancePlanActualCube
  3. Create a calculated measure for Achievement %
  4. Publish as a report/KPI

This is the clean-core approach supported by SAP for analytical calculations. [community.sap.com]

If you're on a specific release (for example 2508/2602) and have access to Custom Analytical Queries, I can give you the exact step-by-step configuration for creating the Achievement % measure.

entry.title: Putaway for batch-managed products without HU
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am currently testing a putaway scenario in SAP EWM Public Cloud and have not been able to find a standard configuration that fully covers the customer's requirement.

The customer has batch-managed products in the finished goods warehouse but does not use Handling Units.

The required putaway logic is as follows:

  1. During putaway, EWM should search the entire storage type for existing stock of the same product.
  2. If the same product already exists in a storage bin, the new stock should be added to this bin regardless of the batch number.
  3. Mixed storage of different products in the same storage bin is allowed.
  4. If no existing stock of the same product can be found anywhere in the storage type, EWM should determine the first suitable empty storage bin.

Example:

  • Bin 01 contains Product A and Product B.
  • Bin 02 is empty.
  • Bin 03 contains Product C.

For an inbound delivery containing Products A, B and C, the expected putaway result is:

  • Product A → Bin 01
  • Product B → Bin 01
  • Product C → Bin 03
  • Product D → empty bin 02 (as there is no existing stock of Product D in the storage type)

 

I tested Storage Behavior = Standard Warehouse with Putaway Rule = Addition to Existing Stock/Empty Bin and mixed storage without restrictions (as well as mixed storage adjusted to point 2). However, for batch-managed products, EWM determines each time separate empty bins instead of adding the stock to bins containing the same product with result:

  • Product A → Bin 02
  • Product B → Bin 04
  • Product C → Bin 05

The only configuration that partially works for this case is Putaway Rule = 4 General Storage Area with the corresponding mixed storage settings, as shown below.

With this setting the system adds stock to existing stock in some cases:

  • Product A → Bin 01 - correct
  • Product B → Bin 01 - correct
  • Product C → Bin 02 - not correct

 

Based on my tests, the system does not seem to search the complete storage type for existing stock of the same product. If the product is not found in the first relevant bin(s), EWM switches to empty-bin determination and selects the first suitable empty bin, even though the same product exists in another bin later in the storage type.

 

My question is:

Is there a standard EWM (Public Cloud) configuration that allows the system to search the complete storage type for existing stock of the same batch-managed product, add the new stock to that bin regardless of the batch number, and only use an empty bin if no stock of the product exists anywhere in the storage type?

Is there an additional setting related to addition to existing stock, mixed storage, stock identification, or putaway sorting that I may have missed?

The scenario is without Handling Units.

Thanks in advance for any advice or configuration hints.

entry.title: Re: How to validate manually entered batch numbers during...
entry.content:
Good day @troy_tange0b22c

Thank you for your question.

 

 

Yes, there are standard options to validate manually entered batch numbers. The better option depends on the type of validation you need.

Option 1:

You can use a standard number range check. If the rule is simply that the manually entered batch number must be within an allowed external number range, you can also use standard batch number configuration. Note that during batch creation or during goods movement, batch numbers can be assigned manually or automatically. Be aware that batch numbers you enter manually can be checked against a number range.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/configuring-supply-chain-business-scenarios-in-sap-s4hana-cloud-public-edition/setting-up-batch-numbers_c0292a39-c418-4025-8fef-ba43cbeb93aa?

Here are configuration activities:

  • Ensure you define Batch numbers Ranges, then define Batch Number Ranges by Plant, if you require plant specific ranges.
  • Thereafter, define initial creation for Batch Master Maintenance.
  • Then make sure you define Batch Creation for Goods movement.

Option 2: You can use Custom Logic for additional validation. If your rule is more specific, for example prefix by product type, plant, date format, supplier code, or country logic, ensure you use Custom Logic. You can use Custom Logic to implement BAdIs and behaviour extension. For your case, check the batch option Extensibility for Batch Number Check.

See the following references for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/3aa57e5b9a9f46218ef8cc38d703c8f4.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/546ac23df1254af3baca5d9afe99ed04.html?locale=en-US

Here is a quick guide:

In Custom Logic, choose Create and search for batch related BAdIs, especially the batch number check option.

Open View Documentation for the BAdI so, you can implement the validation rule and raise an error message when the batch number is invalid.

Make sure you test in Manage Batches, which allows you to display, edit, and create batches.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Custom Field in App: Currency Exchange Rates
entry.content:
"Does Indonesian regulation require storing the KMK reference number inside SAP transactions, or only retaining evidence of which tax exchange rate was applied?"
If it is only an audit/documentation requirement, then a Custom Business Object with attachments is likely sufficient and no BAdI is needed at all. That's the cleanest Public Cloud solution.

entry.title: 1H 2026 SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors - Recent Product Release Highlights
entry.content:
SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors – SuccessFactors Connectivity Hub

On June 15, we delivered a set of SmartRecruiters and SAP SuccessFactors connectivity updates that make setup more centralized, field mapping more flexible, and data handoff more reliable—from position creation through hiring and into Employee Central and Onboarding.

This release introduces a new SuccessFactors Connectivity Hub in SmartRecruiters Settings, enabling administrators to manage key configuration areas for master data sync, Position-to-Job mapping, and Employee Central-related sync flows from one centralized location.

Key Features

  • Centralized Integration Hub: Manage core SmartRecruiters and SuccessFactors mapping configuration from one dedicated settings area.
  • Externally Managed Application Fields: Create SmartRecruiters application fields from SuccessFactors picklists and objects, indicate they are managed in SuccessFactors, and deactivate or reactivate them in SmartRecruiters as needed.
  • Flexible Job and Org Field Mapping: Map the same SuccessFactors object to multiple SmartRecruiters fields, assign custom labels, separate child fields from parent hierarchies, remove mappings that are no longer needed, and avoid full-batch failures when only some records have unsupported configurations.
  • Automatic Job Prefill from Positions: When creating SmartRecruiters jobs from SuccessFactors Positions, mapped job data and hiring manager details are pre-populated automatically.
  • Employee Central and Onboarding Data Handoff: Candidate and job data are pre-populated into Employee Central and Onboarding during hire initiation, reducing manual re-entry and improving the accuracy of hire records.

SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors – Migration Hub

Migration Hub now closes the gap between analysis and action. After field mappings and configurations have been reviewed and finalized in the analysis phase, users can run the migration directly from within Migration Hub—without a manual step between the reviewed configuration and migration into SmartRecruiters. Job fields and application fields, including their mappings and configurations, are then migrated automatically from SAP SuccessFactors to SmartRecruiters.

This release also expands Migration Hub beyond the Configuration Analyzer foundation to cover Application, Candidate, and Offer configurations, giving customers a more complete view of migration readiness in a single place.

Key Features

  • Run Migration from Migration Hub: Users can now trigger migration after completing and reviewing the configuration analysis, eliminating the previous manual handoff step.
  • Application Field Analysis: Migration Hub now analyzes Application fields and sections, including data types and dependencies.
  • Candidate Profile Field Analysis: Candidate profile fields are now analyzed, including field reuse across Job Requisition and Application configurations.
  • Offer Field Analysis: Migration Hub now analyzes Offer fields, including relevant data types and picklist usage.
  • Expanded Migration Guidance: Includes data type mismatch and transformation recommendations, picklist update recommendations, and target-system compatibility insights.
  • Integration Sync Handoff for Eligible Fields: For eligible single-select fields, Migration Hub sends field definitions, supported attributes, picklist values, and dependency relationships directly to Integration Sync so those fields can be created and maintained consistently in SmartRecruiters.

Applicant Data Transfer enhancements

Our June release expands Applicant Data Transferability (ADT) to give administrators fine‑grained control and improve data hygiene and compliance. Admins can set global transfer rules as mandatory or recruiter overridable during Add to Job, while recruiters can choose which elements (e.g., resumes, attachments, internal notes) to move based on context. Screening data is configurable by category—default library questions, job‑specific questions, and DEI/EEO—so only compliant, relevant information is carried forward. Transferred notes are now scoped to the destination application’s Notes tab (not the candidate profile or other applications), with full content preserved for authorized users. These enhancements strengthen governance, flexibility, and data integrity, especially for multi‑brand, global, and decentralized teams.

Winston Match – Candidate-Verified Data Prioritization

Winston Match now prioritizes candidate-verified profile data over AI-extracted resume content when calculating match scores. When applicants confirm or manually enter their work experience and education in their profile, that information becomes the primary source for scoring. Resume parsing is used only as a fallback when profile data is missing. This update improves score accuracy, strengthens human oversight, and supports more responsible AI-assisted hiring.

How It Works

Profile Data Precedence

When an applicant has at least one confirmed or manually entered experience or education entry, Winston Match uses that candidate-verified data for scoring.

Independent Sources per Section

Experience and education are evaluated separately. Verified experience always takes precedence for experience signals; verified education for education signals.

Defined Profile Fields

Work history: title, employer name, start date, end date, and summary. Education: degree or certification, duration, and degree description.

Fallback Behavior

If profile data is unavailable for a section, Winston Match falls back to AI-extracted resume content. If neither source has enough data, no low-confidence score is generated.

Summary

One release, four big wins: easier integrations, click‑to‑migrate, cleaner transfers, and smarter matching. The Connectivity Hub and Migration Hub cut setup and handoffs, ADT keeps your candidate data clean and compliant, and Winston Match boosts accuracy with candidate‑verified profiles. Hire faster, govern smarter, and scale with confidence.

Helpful links:

Whats new viewer: https://help.sap.com/docs/SMARTRECRUITERS_FOR_SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS?locale=en-US#discover_task-whats_new

entry.title: How to trigger automatic email notifications to customers upon receipt of payment (Public Cloud)?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I have a requirement from a client using SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition version 2602

The Requirement

The client wants an automatic email notification to be triggered and sent to the customer as soon as a payment is received and posted in the system.

  • This should apply to all types of payments, including on-account payments, advance payments, and invoice payments.

  • The email should dynamically state the received amount and currency (e.g., "We have successfully received your payment of [Amount] [Currency]").

My Questions

  1. Is there a standard, out-of-the-box configuration in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to achieve this?

  2. Can this be achieved using SAP S/4HANA Output Management (e.g., triggering an email via the payment journal/clearing document posting)?

  3. If a standard solution does not exist, what are the recommended workarounds? (e.g., utilizing SAP Event Mesh, SAP Integration Suite, or a custom BAdI on journal entry creation?)

I would highly appreciate any step-by-step guidance, configuration paths, or best practices you can share to fulfill this setup.

Thank you in advance for your help!

Best regards,

entry.title: SmartRecruiters and SAP SuccessFactors Connectivity
entry.content:
When we published the SmartRecruiters and SAP SuccessFactors Roadmap in H1, we committed to building a recruiting experience that felt native — not bolted on. The end of H1 2026 marked the first complete delivery against that commitment. This blog covers everything that shipped: what it does, how it works, and what it means for customers transitioning from SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting to SmartRecruiters. For more detail, please refer to the linked help.sap pdfs.

What the Connectivity Delivers

Four foundational commitments govern the integration:

  • Unified Login  SmartRecruiters authenticates via SAP IAS/SSO — one login for the full suite, no separate SR password.
  • Consistent UX  SmartRecruiters renders as a native SuccessFactors module with an SAP-aligned shell bar; Latest SAP Experience aligns automatically.
  • Single Source of Truth  Identity, org structures, job data, and employee records are mastered in SF and synced to SR. Manual SR user creation is disabled.
  • Position-Led Hiring  Requisitions in SR are created from approved SF positions, keeping headcount authority in SuccessFactors.

Implementation Journey & Data Flows

Seven-Step Journey - Implementation follows two phases: the Foundation Phase (steps 1–4) establishes identity, data, and experience; the Hire-to-Employee Phase (steps 5–7) connects recruiting decisions back to core HR.

 

Step

What Happens

1 — Landscape & Provisioning

Provision environments and pair each SF instance 1:1 with a SmartRecruiters instance. A Super Admin system user is created at provisioning. Requires an authorized SAP partner consultant.

2 — Identity, SSO & User Sync

Configure SAP IAS so SuccessFactors credentials provide unified login. Initial user sync creates all SF users in SmartRecruiters with the default Employee role. User creation and deactivation are managed in SuccessFactors.

3 — Foundational Data Sync

Sync foundation objects (locations, departments, legal entities, pay grades) from SuccessFactors. These become externally managed, read-only Job and Org fields in SmartRecruiters.

4 — Unified UX

SmartRecruiters appears as a native product entry point in SF navigation with an SAP-aligned shell bar. SSO sign-in is automatic. Customers on Latest SAP Experience see automatic visual alignment.

5 — Field Mapping (Integration Hub)

Configure Position → SR Job and SR → EC/Onboarding field mappings centrally in the SuccessFactors Integration Hub. Not mandatory, but significantly reduces downstream manual effort.

6 — Position → SmartRecruiters Job

Authorized users create a job from an approved SF position in the Org Chart. The draft is pre-populated via Integration Hub mappings. A two-way position-job reference is maintained throughout the hiring process.

7 — Send Hires to EC & Onboarding

Recruiter initiates a hire; candidate data flows automatically to EC (Manage Pending Hires) or the Onboarding Dashboard. HR admin reviews, adjusts, and confirms to create the employee record.

Five Bi-directional Data Flows - The connectivity is underpinned by five bi-directional data flows. Three run from SuccessFactors to SmartRecruiters; two run in the reverse direction. Together they form a complete data loop from workforce planning through to employee creation.

 

Flow

Direction

What It Does

User Sync

SF → SR

Full user lifecycle (create, update, deactivate) governed in SuccessFactors. Required fields: email, firstName, lastName, userName, personUUID, status. ~1 hour propagation.

Org & Master Data

SF → SR

Foundation objects and picklists sync as externally managed Job, Org, and Application fields. Read-only in SmartRecruiters. Scheduled twice per 24 hours; force sync available on-demand.

Position → Job Draft

SF → SR

Approved positions drive SmartRecruiters job creation. Integration Hub mappings pre-populate the draft. Position ID and Job Title always transfer. Hiring Manager derived from parent position in the reporting hierarchy.

Hire Sync

SR → SF

Recruiter-initiated hire pushes candidate data to EC (Manage Pending Hires) or the Onboarding Dashboard. Mandatory fields validated before transfer.

Employee Sync Back

SF → SR

Once an employee record is created in EC, a SmartRecruiters user profile is auto-created with the Employee role and SSO configured.

Tooling: Integration Hub & Migration Hub

Integration Hub (Settings → Administration → SF Integration Hub) 

Single configuration point for all field mappings across three areas:

  • SF foundation objects synced into SR as externally managed Job/Org fields (prerequisite for the other two areas).
  • SF Position fields mapped to SR Job/Org fields (Text, Foundation Object, Generic Object, Picklist). Position ID and Job Title always transfer.
  • SR Job and Application fields mapped to EC fields — First Name, Last Name, Email, Legal Entity, Hiring Manager, and Hire Date are pre-configured.

Migration Hub  (Settings → Administration → Migration Hub)

Guides customers from SF Recruiting ATS configuration to SR. Surfaces the SF field inventory with type, 12-month usage data, and compatibility status. Supports plan → configure → execute workflows category by category. The June 2026 release is the most significant update since launch.

Career Site Builder for SmartRecruiters

H1 2026 delivers Career Site Builder for customers who have completed initial SR–SF connectivity. It provides a responsive, fully brandable career site where external candidates can search and apply. Jobs sync nightly; manual full sync is available on demand.

Single ATS Mode Only

Productive use with SmartRecruiters ATS exclusively. Once integrated, SF Recruiting job postings no longer appear on the career site. Dual ATS mode is not available in this release.

Prerequisites

  • SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors license.
  • User sync and foundational data sync between SF and SR active.
  • Provisioned CSB instance with Recruiting, Recruiting Marketing, Enable Job Marketing, CSB, and Candidate Account Simplification enabled in Provisioning.
  • Job Location object configured in SF with recurring data sync to SR.
  • Unified Data Model enabled (auto-enabled on CSB activation if not already active).

Future Roadmap— Career Site Builder

  • Dual ATS mode; Internal Career Site; private job postings.
  • Real-time job sync (nightly schedule or manual full sync only).
  • Job alerts; source/campaign tracking; advanced analytics.
  • AI-assisted skills matching, Similar Jobs, Skills Cloud components.
  • Data Capture Forms and Lead Capture Forms.
  • Branded apply experience within the SR One-Click Apply flow.
  • SR Sandbox integration via CSB UI (must be configured manually by SAP).
  • Employee referral workflows on the career site (available via SR Employee Portal).

Coexistence Models: Managing the Transition

Governing principle: one requisition, one system — no exceptions.

Full Company Cutover

Single defined cutover date for the whole organization. No new SF Recruiting requisitions after that date; open ones run to natural close. Optional coexistence window of 30, 60, or 90 days per persona.

Phase-Based Rollout

Gradual migration by country, legal entity, division, or department — each phase with its own cutover date. Dynamic permission groups scoped to employee attributes auto-expire legacy access at a defined point.

Governance: Native RBP, No Middleware

Coexistence is governed entirely through SF Role-Based Permissions and dynamic permission groups. Effective dates auto-expire legacy access. No custom middleware required.

What’s Not Feasible with the Coexistence Model

  • Tehnical migration of requisitions, candidates, or configuration.
  • Requisition sync — open SF Recruiting requisitions are not visible in SR.
  • Candidate record merge — profiles are fully system-contained, no deduplication.
  • Shared approvals or unified reporting across platforms.

Managing User Access

Externally Managed Users

Users created by the connectivity are externally managed. Core identity (name, email, SSO ID, language, timezone) is locked in SR and can only be changed via SF (propagates ~1 hour). Admins can still configure System Role, Access Group, and Hiring Team Role in SR. Deactivation is also controlled by SF.

Externally Managed Fields

Foundation objects synced from SF become externally managed fields in SR — usable in workflows and reporting, but cannot be renamed, retyped, or recategorized. Structural changes must originate in SF.

Side-by-Side Access (Testing/Warm-Up Only)

Selected users can be granted early SR access while SF Recruiting remains active — for controlled testing only, not production recruiting. Requires explicit RBP assignment. The Module Picker will show both SR and SF Recruiting as separate entries with identical icons. Dual access must be limited to a clearly defined group with a defined end date.

What Is Not Yet Available — H1 2026

  • SR reporting within SF Story reporting or cross-module analytics.
  • Internal candidates, rehires, global assignments, and concurrent employment pre-population.
  • Candidate attachment transfer to Employee Central or Onboarding.
  • Retry mechanism for failed hire scenarios; syncing candidate updates made after hire initiation.
  • Country-specific EC entity field configuration in mapping pages.
  • Migration of historical recruiting data (requisitions, candidates, applications).

Summary

In H1 2026, SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors turns integration into business value: recruiters and HR work in a single, familiar SAP experience with one login, hiring moves faster with fewer handoffs and less re-entry, and headcount stays tightly governed in SuccessFactors for clean, compliant control. Centralized configuration and automated data flows reduce implementation risk and operational overhead, while position‑led hiring and hire‑to‑EC/Onboarding handoff increase data accuracy and auditability. Customers can phase adoption without middleware, keep teams productive during cutover, and standardize on a single source of truth—resulting in quicker time to value, stronger compliance, lower total cost of ownership, and a smoother transition from SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting to SmartRecruiters.

Useful links:

Implementation guidance - https://help.sap.com/docs/SMARTRECRUITERS_FOR_SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS?locale=en-US#implement_task-implementation

Whats new viewer - https://help.sap.com/docs/SMARTRECRUITERS_FOR_SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS?locale=en-US#discover_task-whats_new

Integration playbook- https://help.sap.com/docs/link-disclaimer?site=https%3A%2F%2Fcustomers.smartrecruiters.com%2Fkb%2Farticles%2F5940-smartrecruiters-for-sap-successfactors-implementation-playbook

entry.title: Consumption Tax (Input/Output VAT) in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Complete Posting Flow from Invo
entry.content:
Consumption Tax (Input/Output VAT) in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Complete Posting Flow from Invoice to Clearing — Which SSCUI Configures What?

**Product:** SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (2508 / 2602 / 2608)
**Area:** Finance — General Ledger / AR / AP / Tax
**Country/Region:** Japan (JP), Chart of Accounts **YCOA**

---

## Background

I'm an FI consultant currently running Fit-to-Standard workshops for a Japanese customer on S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The customer asked what I thought was a simple question:

> "Walk us through every journal entry involving consumption tax, from invoice creation through to clearing. And tell us exactly where each one is configured."

I couldn't find a single consolidated source for this, so I pieced it together myself. Below is what I've established. I'd appreciate corrections and answers to the open questions at the end.

A recurring misconception I want to address up front, because I initially fell for a variant of it myself:

> **Consumption tax does NOT move at payment clearing.**

There is no "behind the scenes" tax transfer at clearing the way there is with, say, prepaid expense amortisation. Japan taxes on an accrual basis, so SAP finalises the tax amount at invoice posting and the balance simply sits on the balance sheet until it is manually transferred at declaration time.

---

## 1. Summary Table

| # | Event | Tax posted? | Accounts involved |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Down payment **request** (Sp. G/L ind. **F**) | **No** — noted item, no G/L posting at all | — |
| 2 | Down payment (Sp. G/L ind. **A**) | **No in Japan** (config-dependent) | MVA/VVA mechanism exists for other countries |
| 3 | **Customer invoice posting** | **Yes** | Output tax (**MWS**) |
| 4 | **Supplier invoice posting** | **Yes** | Input tax (**VST**) |
| 5 | Incoming / outgoing payment clearing | **No** | — |
| 6 | Clearing with cash discount | Yes (tax adjustment) | Cash discount + tax adjustment |
| 7 | Foreign currency clearing | **No** (tax fixed in document currency) | Exchange rate difference (KDF) only |
| 8 | **Tax declaration transfer** | **Yes (manual)** | Output/Input tax → Tax payable |

---

## 2. Normal AR / AP Cycle

### 2.1 Output Tax — Transaction Key **MWS**

**Step 1: Invoice posting** (`Create Billing Documents` / `Create Outgoing Invoices`)

```
Dr Accounts Receivable 1,100
Cr Revenue 1,000
Cr Output Tax (MWS) 100
```

Determination path for SD-driven postings:

```
Condition type (MWST etc.)
→ Account key MWS
→ G/L account (Output Tax)
```

For direct FI postings, the tax code entered on the line item resolves MWS → G/L account directly.

**Step 2: Incoming payment clearing** (`Post Incoming Payments`)

```
Dr Bank 1,100
Cr Accounts Receivable 1,100
```

That's it. **Output tax does not appear.**

### 2.2 Input Tax — Transaction Key **VST**

**Step 1: Supplier invoice posting** (`Create Supplier Invoice` / `Create Incoming Invoices`)

```
Dr Purchases / Expense 1,000
Dr Input Tax (VST) 100
Cr Accounts Payable 1,100
```

**Step 2: Outgoing payment clearing** (`Post Outgoing Payments` / `Manage Automatic Payments`)

```
Dr Accounts Payable 1,100
Cr Bank 1,100
```

Again — **no tax movement.**

---

## 3. The Exceptions (where tax *does* move outside invoice posting)

### 3.1 Down Payment Tax Clearing — **MVA / VVA**

This is the mechanism people are usually thinking of when they intuit "there must be an offsetting tax account somewhere."

**First, an important distinction:**

A **down payment request** (Sp. G/L indicator **F**) is a **noted item**. Per SAP Help: it functions as a reference document for the down payment posting or as a document for the dunning program, and **account balances are not updated**. Neither transaction figures nor account balances are managed on these accounts — you can only view them via line item display.

So a DP request posts **nothing at all** — not tax, not receivables. This is standard behaviour worldwide, not a Japanese specialty. A tax code *is* entered and carried forward to the subsequent down payment posting, but nothing hits the G/L.

**The actual down payment** (Sp. G/L indicator **A**) is where the tax clearing account appears — in countries where a down payment triggers a VAT liability (Germany, Russia, Ukraine etc.):

```
[Step 1] Down payment received
Dr Bank 1,100
Cr Down Payment Received (Sp.G/L A) 1,100
Dr Output Tax Clearing (MVA) 100 ← the offsetting account
Cr Output Tax (MWS) 100

[Step 2] Final invoice
Dr Accounts Receivable 1,100
Cr Revenue 1,000
Cr Output Tax (MWS) 100 ← full amount

[Step 3] Down payment clearing
Dr Down Payment Received 1,100
Cr Accounts Receivable 1,100
Dr Output Tax (MWS) 100
Cr Output Tax Clearing (MVA) 100 ← reverses Step 1
```

Net result: output tax = 100 (final invoice only). The clearing account is a **buffer that prevents double counting**.

| Key | Meaning |
|---|---|
| **MVA** | Output tax clearing on down payments |
| **VVA** | Input tax clearing on down payments |

**For Japan:** down payments received/made are **outside the scope of consumption tax** — tax is triggered by the transfer of assets or provision of services, i.e. at the final invoice. So MVA/VVA are not used. You suppress the tax posting by leaving the **tax category blank on the down payment alternative reconciliation account**, or by assigning a **non-taxable tax code** to the DP request.

SAP explicitly supports the latter pattern — see the Help topic *"How to Set Deviating Tax Codes in Down Payment Requests for Intracommunity Supply"* (ensures no output tax on the DP request while the clearing invoice is taxed with a different code). It's written for EU intracommunity supply, but the "no tax at DP, full tax at final invoice" logic maps directly onto the Japanese requirement.

**Gotcha:** if the DP reconciliation account has tax category `*`, entering a tax code will fail with an account determination error (`FH145` family) unless MVA/VVA are maintained.

**Resulting Japanese flow:**

```
① DP request (F) → no posting (noted item only)
② DP received Dr Bank 1,100 / Cr Down Payment Received 1,100 ← no tax
③ Final invoice Dr AR 1,100 / Cr Revenue 1,000
Cr Output Tax 100 ← full amount here
④ DP clearing Dr Down Payment Received 1,100 / Cr AR 1,100 ← no tax
```

### 3.2 Cash Discount

Per SAP Help, the G/L account configured for cash discount expense/revenue **controls whether a tax adjustment is made**.

```
Dr Accounts Payable 1,100
Cr Bank 1,078
Cr Cash Discount Received 20
Cr Input Tax (VST) 2 ← tax adjustment
```

Rarely used in Japanese business practice, but if used, tax **does** move at clearing. Note also that reversing a clearing where a cash discount triggered a tax adjustment requires a clearing reset — the procedure is more involved than a plain reversal.

### 3.3 Deferred Tax — **not applicable to Japan**

Per SAP Help: in some countries tax is not recognised at invoice issue but only when payment is made; this is called deferred tax, and the system can record and transfer it (dedicated "Deferred Tax Transfer" app).

This is the one genuine "tax moves at clearing" mechanism — used for cash-basis countries (Italy, France, Thailand). **Japan is accrual-basis, so it does not apply.**

### 3.4 Non-Deductible Input Tax — **NAV / NVV**

| Key | Behaviour | Account determination |
|---|---|---|
| **NAV** | Non-deductible input tax posted to a **separate account** | Required |
| **NVV** | Non-deductible input tax **distributed to the expense/asset** | **Not required** |

Per KBA 3263224: NVV is not available in SSCUI 100297 by design — the tax is calculated technically but is not posted to a separate account, so no account determination is needed.

```
[NVV]
Dr Expense 1,100 ← tax absorbed into cost
Cr Accounts Payable 1,100
```

This trips people up — the absence of NVV in 100297 looks like a config gap but isn't.

### 3.5 Foreign Currency / Write-Off

- **FX clearing:** tax is fixed in document currency; only exchange rate difference (KDF) posts. No tax movement.
- **Write-off:** loss posts, but consumption tax adjustment is handled separately (in Japan, bad-debt VAT relief is a declaration-level adjustment).

---

## 4. Tax Declaration Transfer — the actual "tax clearing"

Per SAP Help (*Reporting to the Tax Authority*), there are two methods to credit the tax accounts by transferring amounts to a tax payable account:

**Method 1 — via the Advance Return for Tax program**

The `Advance Return for Tax` job creates a batch input session for automatic transfer posting of tax payable. Per Help: when the session is created, one document is prepared per selected country code, containing one item posting to the tax account and one item posting to the tax payable account, for each tax account.

The tax payable account for this is configured in **SSCUI 100297**:

```
Area : Financial Accounting
Subarea : General Ledger Accounting
Process : Define Tax Accounts
→ Step 2
Transaction Group : STE (Miscellaneous Taxes)
Transaction Key : UMS
Chart of Accounts : YCOA
→ Step 3 → assign the Tax Payable G/L account
```

(This exact path is documented in the Help topic *"Making Additional Settings for VAT Return"* — written for Ukraine, but the SSCUI navigation is generic.)

**Method 2 — manual posting via `Post Tax Payables` (App ID **F1597**)** ← *the approach we're adopting*

Per SAP Help: you use this app to pay tax amounts by posting the balances of input and output tax accounts to a tax payable account. **The app does not require entry of a tax code**, enabling direct manual adjustments to tax accounts.

Key features: save a draft before posting; post tax account balances to a tax payable account; add attachments and notes explaining the reason for the posting.

```
Dr Output Tax 1,000
Cr Input Tax 700
Cr Tax Payable 300 ← amount due
```

### ⚠️ 4.1 F1597 account visibility — the biggest trap I found

Per SAP Help, the settings in `Manage G/L Account Master Data` (SSCUI **150001**) determine whether a G/L account can be found in the `Post Tax Payables` app. **The search result does NOT include the account if any of the following apply:**

- the account is blocked for posting
- the reconciliation type has a value
- **the account is "only automatically posted"**
- the account is not "only automatically posted" **and the tax category has a value other than `>` or `<`**

So to use F1597 on your tax accounts:

| Setting | Required value |
|---|---|
| Blocked for posting | No |
| Reconciliation type | Blank |
| **Only automatically posted** | **Unchecked** |
| **Tax category** | Output tax = `>` / Input tax = `<` |

**This is a direct conflict with common practice.** Tax accounts are usually flagged "only automatically posted" to prevent manual entry — but doing so hides them from F1597. If you want F1597, you must uncheck it, which means the accounts become manually postable from journal entry apps. **Control has to be handled via authorizations (business roles/catalogs) instead.** Worth raising with the customer's internal controls team early.

Also per Help:
- **The tax payable account itself must NOT be tax-relevant** (tax category blank).
- **The tax amounts are not included in the advance report for the following month**, since the generated journal entries contain no tax information. So no double counting — this one is reassuring.

### 4.2 Japan VAT Return

```
Reporting entity : JP_RPG_ENT (Japan Reporting Entity)
Report category : JP_VAT_RETURN (Japan VAT Return)
App : Run Statutory Reports
```

Reporting activities: **`Manage Tax Items`** → **`Prepare VAT Return (Japan)`**.

**Notably, `Tax Payable Posting` is NOT among the reporting activities defined for Japan** — unlike, for example, the Italian or Ukrainian VAT returns, where it is. This appears to confirm that for Japan, SAP expects the tax payable transfer to be done manually via F1597 or a manual journal entry.

**Prerequisite that's easy to miss:** per Help, make sure the **tax reporting date** of financial documents is activated at company code level in the `Enter Global Parameters` configuration activity. For legacy financial documents where the tax reporting date is empty, **you must enter the date manually** — relevant if you're migrating open items via the Migration Cockpit.

---

## 5. SSCUI Map

| # | What | SSCUI / Activity | Location | On-prem equivalent |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Tax codes** | **101016** — Define Tax Codes for Sales and Purchases | CBC | FTXP |
| 2 | **Tax accounts (MWS/VST/NAV/ESA/ESE)** | **100297** — Automatic Account Determination | Area: Financial Accounting → Subarea: **General Ledger Accounting** → Process: **Define Tax Accounts** → Transaction Group **%TX** | OB40 |
| 3 | **Tax payable account (UMS)** | **100297** | as above, Transaction Group **STE** → Key **UMS** | — |
| 4 | **DP tax clearing (MVA/VVA)** | **100297** *(assumed — see Q1)* | assumed under Subarea: Accounts Receivable / Accounts Payable | **OBXB** |
| 5 | **DP alternative recon. accounts** | SSCUI (Define Recon. Accounts for Customer/Supplier Down Payments) | — | OBXR / OBYR |
| 6 | **G/L master (tax category, auto-post flag)** | **150001** — Manage G/L Account Master Data | — | FS00 |
| 7 | **Tax reporting date activation** | Enter Global Parameters | Company code | OBY6 |
| 8 | **VAT return setup** | Setting Up Your Statutory Reporting / Configure Rules for VAT Return | JP_RPG_ENT / JP_VAT_RETURN | — |

### Transaction key reference

| Group | Key | Description | Account determination |
|---|---|---|---|
| %TX | **MWS** | Output tax | Required |
| %TX | **VST** | Input tax | Required |
| %TX | **NAV** | Non-deductible input tax (separate account) | Required if used |
| %TX | **NVV** | Non-deductible input tax (distributed to cost) | **Not required — by design** |
| %TX | **ESA / ESE** | Acquisition tax (EU) | Not needed for JP |
| **STE** | **UMS** | Tax payable transfer | Only if using automatic transfer |
| — | **MVA** | Output tax clearing on down payments | Not needed for JP |
| — | **VVA** | Input tax clearing on down payments | Not needed for JP |

### 100297 navigation (worked example, per KBA 3016325)

```
1. Open Fiori app "Manage Your Solution"
2. Search for 100297 (Automatic Account Determination) → Configure
3. [Step 1] Area : Financial Accounting
Subarea : General Ledger Accounting
Process : Define Tax Accounts
4. [Step 2] Transaction Group : %TX (Tax on Sales/Purchases)
Transaction Key : VST
Chart of Accounts : YCOA
Tax Code : (your code)
Country : JP
5. [Step 3] Add → enter G/L account
6. Review → Save
```

**Tip:** the Step 3 screen has an "Export to Excel" option — it exports all entries for the selected category even if nothing was changed, which is useful for design document evidence. There's also a released CDS view `I_TRANSACTIONTYPESTDGLACCT` that can be used as a base for a custom view to extract current settings (I haven't verified this myself).

### YCOA accounts

The only YCOA account number I can cite with confidence is **12600000 (Input Tax)**, which appears as the worked example in KBA 3016325. I've deliberately left the others as placeholders rather than guess — see Q4 below.

| Purpose | Transaction key | YCOA account |
|---|---|---|
| Input Tax | VST | **12600000** (per KBA 3016325) |
| Output Tax | MWS | *(to be confirmed)* |
| Tax Payable | UMS | *(to be confirmed)* |
| Non-deductible input tax | NAV | *(to be confirmed)* |

---

## 6. Common Pitfalls (summary)

1. **Create a tax code in 101016 but forget 100297** → `Error in account determination: table T030K key YCOA MWS JP` (see KBA 3339321).
2. **Expecting NVV in 100297** → it's absent by design (KBA 3263224).
3. **Tax accounts flagged "only automatically posted"** → invisible in F1597 (§4.1).
4. **Tax payable account flagged tax-relevant** → rejected by F1597.
5. **Tax reporting date not activated / empty on migrated documents** → items won't appear in the VAT return.
6. **DP reconciliation account with tax category `*` in Japan** → account determination errors for unused MVA/VVA.
7. **Telling the customer the tax settlement is automatic** → it isn't. Set this expectation in Fit-to-Standard, not in UAT.

---

## 7. Open Questions

I'd be grateful for input on the following:

1. **What is the exact SSCUI path for MVA / VVA in Public Edition?**
On-prem it's `OBXB` (SPRO → FI → AR/AP → Business Transactions → Down Payments Made/Received → Define Account for Tax Clearing). I could not find documentation confirming which Subarea/Process exposes these in SSCUI 100297. I've assumed *Subarea: Accounts Receivable / Accounts Payable*. Has anyone maintained these in Public Edition and can confirm?

2. **Can `Tax Payable Posting` be added as a reporting activity to JP_VAT_RETURN?**
Is the activity list fixed by SAP-delivered content, or can it be extended via `Setting Up Your Statutory Reporting`? If it can be added, that would provide an automatic transfer path for Japan.

3. **How are others controlling F1597 access without the "only automatically posted" flag?**
Is business role segregation the only lever, or has anyone found a cleaner approach to protecting tax accounts from manual entry while keeping F1597 usable?

4. **Can anyone confirm the standard YCOA account numbers** for Output Tax (MWS), Tax Payable (UMS), and Non-deductible Input Tax (NAV) in the current Japan Best Practices content? I only have 12600000 for Input Tax from KBA 3016325.

5. **Reduced rate (8%) vs standard rate (10%) — separate G/L accounts?**
100297 allows account determination per tax code, so splitting is technically possible. But since the VAT return aggregates by tax code, I've been advising customers that separate accounts aren't necessary. Has anyone implemented separate accounts, and what drove that decision?

---

## References

**SAP Help Portal — S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition**
Post Tax Payables (F1597) · Advance Return for Tax · Reporting to the Tax Authority · Making Additional Settings for VAT Return · VAT Return (Japan) · Configuration for VAT Return (Japan) · Down Payment Requests · Post Customer Down Payment Requests · Deferred Tax · Cash Discount in Finance · Automatic Postings During Clearing · How to Set Deviating Tax Codes in Down Payment Requests for Intracommunity Supply

**SAP Notes / KBAs**
3009061 (tax code creation, SSCUI 101016) · 3016325 (assigning G/L to tax codes, SSCUI 100297) · 3339321 (T030K account determination error) · 3263224 (NVV in SSCUI 100297) · 2987771 (checking automatic account determination for a G/L account)

---

*Corrections welcome — particularly on the open questions above. Happy to update this post with confirmed answers so it's useful as a reference.*

entry.title: Lock net price and amount in condition tab in PO document in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

Can we lock the net price and amount in condition tab in PO document in Public Cloud(T-code ME21N or App Manage Purchase Order in creation/modification Mode)?

the purpose I want to lock the price is to prevent the user from straightaway changing the price in PO, they should maintain the price in info record not straightaway from PO document.

Previously in Private Cloud Edition, we are setting this kind of required use "define screen layout at document level" to customize a Z field selection group and define display price only, and then assign to puchase document type.

 

But now I cannot find above IMG or SSCUI in Public cloud to define screen layout control, but I still can assign document type field group in below screen, can you guide me how to do?

 

 

 

Thanks

Tina

entry.title: Need to Restrict Ad Hoc Moves from GI-ZONE to Regular Storage Bins without cancpick process
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have identified a process gap in SAP EWM and are looking for recommendations on the best way to address it.

Current Situation

SAP EWM currently allows users to perform ad hoc warehouse tasks to move stock from a GI-ZONE (Goods Issue staging area) back into a regular storage bin.

Operationally, this is causing issues because forklift operators sometimes move picked pallets from the GI-ZONE back into racking locations without executing the proper Cancel Pick process. As a result, the physical stock movement does not align with the intended EWM process, creating inventory and process inconsistencies.

Desired Behavior

We would like to enforce the following restrictions:

✅ Allow ad hoc movements:

  • Between regular storage bins
  • Between GI-ZONE bins (one GI-ZONE to another GI-ZONE)

❌ Do not allow ad hoc movements:

  • From GI-ZONE bins to regular storage bins

The expectation is that any stock already staged in the GI-ZONE and requiring return to storage should only be moved through the standard Cancel Pick / Pick Reversal process.

Thanks,

Rag

entry.title: Re: Guide to the Return STO Process in SAP Cloud ERP
entry.content:
Thanks for the information.

However, I still have one question.

Why do we need to create a separate Return STO PO from Plant (B) to Plant (A) instead of creating a normal STO PO from Plant(B) to Plant (A)?

If we create a Return STO PO, is there any link or tracking available between the original STO PO and the Return STO PO?

I couldn't find any information showing that above blog.

So, wouldn't it be better to create a normal STO PO from Plant(B) to Plant (A) instead of using a Return STO PO?

entry.title: Re: Copying the files to my source tree
entry.content: Short answer is you cannot deliver any file or component from the Developer Edition. That likely means you cannot deliver any binary that uses dbcapi without ensuring that the end user (production) has a suitable SQLA license fit for purpose. If that user, for example is also using the Developer Edition, your solution could not be used as it needs a deployment license. This is a sufficiently complex licensing question that I would recommend working with someone with more in-depth licensing knowledge.

entry.title: ASN quantity in Cumulative received Qty in the inbound DELFOR IDOC
entry.content:
Example Scheduling agreement in P01 - 1685053482

Example -

Cumulative delivered qty - 1000

Actually received by customer (GR done) - 600

ASN received by customer, still in transit - 200

In transit                                                  - 200

Customer sends cumulative received qty as 800. This is contrary to SAP design. How do we resolve this?

entry.title: Hide "View as of" from New Full People Profile
entry.content:
Hi team,
Looking for a way to control the visibility of this "As of Today" button:

The reason is: Employees can tell if they are going to be terminated or not based on that output.
They do not have rights to view future dated transactions but....

If this person selects to view info. as of December 31st, their Job and Position Information are displayed as Blank.
This gives the hint of an employee not being part of the organization in the near future.

They do not see the Termination Event or anything, but JobInfo becoming blanks tells them something is off...
Actually, you could play with that As Of date, to pinpoint the exact termination date.

Looking for options to hide or remove this button for certain RBPs.

entry.title: SAP Activate, AI and Data Management
entry.content:
Using AI to transform data during an ERP implementation can significantly reduce effort, improve data quality, and accelerate deployment. ETL tool providers are rapidly infusing their platforms with AI capabilities to automate data extraction, transformation, cleansing, and validation. While those capabilities are best covered by the data migration tool experts, AI also has an important role to play within SAP Activate and Joule for Consultants.

From an SAP Activate perspective, AI extends beyond data transformation by helping implementation teams define data requirements, document project deliverables, and streamline project management activities. We have developed a growing library of AI-assisted prompts that help consultants create implementation artifacts such as migration strategies, identify required migration objects, and generate cutover plans.

Although the examples below are from the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition roadmap, these AI-assisted procedures are available across several SAP product roadmaps. In most cases, the prompts can be adapted simply by changing the SAP product referenced in the instructions.

Examples from the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Roadmap

 Develop a Data Migration Strategy

  • Generate a sample Data Migration Strategy template.
  • Task: Develop the Data Migration Approach and Plan

Identify Required Data Objects

  • Prepare customer master data for the Fit-to-standard workshop.
  • Task: Verify Data and Settings for Fit-to-Standard Workshops for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Determine the migration objects required based on the implementation scope.
  • Task: Determine the Required Data for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Build Cutover Plans

  • Generate a sample list of cutover tasks.
  • Task: Create Cutover Plan and Documentation

We will continue expanding and refining these AI-assisted procedures as new implementation scenarios are identified. To explore the available prompts for Data Migration, open the Road Map Viewer and filter the tasks using the AI-Assisted Procedure tag.

The AI-Assisted Procedure can be identified by the purple arrowhead. Upon clicking the arrowhead, the full AI-Assisted procedure will be revealed.

Give it try!!!

entry.title: Re: Executive Review Import - No update after manual change
entry.content: @Aukasz4 Are there custom validations with a hard stop in your template ? Have you looked at all the content on this blog ? https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/understanding-the-executive-review-import-feature/ba-p/13506522 I'll try to replicate.

entry.title: Data Cleansing of SAP Standard Tables by Removing Special Characters and Spaces in S/4HANA 2023 SP02
entry.content:
Special characters and unintended whitespace are currently being stored in text fields within standard database tables. These non-printable or hidden characters are not readily visible at the database level, making them difficult to identify during data validation and analysis.

However, when records containing these characters are extracted and transmitted to external systems, they cause interface processing failures and data integration issues. 

Do SAP provide any tools or app's or programs that can detect hidden special characters within database fields and support their removal or cleansing?

entry.title: Re: Configuring Reason Codes and Action Codes for France B2B Supplier Invoices in SAP
entry.content:
Hi @Sulafah ,

Yes, this is normal and your XML is correct. The description is not expected to be transferred in the XML. Only the configured reason code and action code is sent as part of the XML payload.

The description is maintained purely for user readability within the configuration.

entry.title: Re: Hilal Kurt
entry.content:
Hi, @hilalkurt

entry.title: Stock discrepancy in WE-ZONE (Storage Type 9010) after complete Putaway confirmation in Embeded EWM
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are facing a stock inconsistency issue in our Embeded EWM system related to Inbound Delivery processing and Putaway.

Process Flow:

  1. Inbound Delivery (IBD) is distributed from ERP to EWM (WMS).
  2. Goods Receipt (GR) is posted successfully against the IBD.
  3. Putaway task (Warehouse Task) is created and confirmed for the full quantity.
  4. As per the process design, stock should move completely from the GR/Interim area (WE-ZONE, Storage Type 9010) to the final destination Storage Bin in the destination Storage Type.

Issue:
Even though the Warehouse Task confirmation shows the Putaway as fully completed (100% quantity confirmed, no open/partially confirmed WTs), the physical stock overview (/SCWM/MON) still shows a residual/duplicate quantity lying in Storage Type 9010 (WE-ZONE — interim GR area) with "0" quantity, in addition to the stock correctly reflected in the destination bin.

This is resulting in inflated stock quantity for the material, as the same stock appears to exist in two locations simultaneously — the interim GR Zone (9010) and the final Putaway bin — even though only one physical movement (Putaway) was performed and confirmed.

Steps Already Performed:

  • Verified Warehouse Task confirmation status – shows "Confirmed," no open quantity.
  • Checked Physical Inventory / Stock overview (/SCWM/MON) – quant still exists in 9010.

    Thanks & regards,
    Saikumar

entry.title: Re: Career Roadmap: Seeking the Definitive Learning Order...
entry.content:
Dear @chris24-dev,

1. Learn the S/4HANA foundations

Start with organizational structures, Business Partner, material master, SAP Fiori, document flow and the differences between:

  • Public Edition: Fit-to-Standard, scope items, CBC and released extensibility
  • Private Edition: broader Customizing and greater process flexibility

More details below:

SAP Learning – Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Sourcing and Procurement

SAP Learning – Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Sourcing and Procurement

2. Master MM and the complete Procure-to-Pay process

Learn purchase requisitions, purchase orders, goods receipts, invoice verification, returns, stock transport orders, subcontracting and consignment

Do not focus only on transactions. You should understand the master data, configuration, logistics documents and accounting postings behind each step

More details below:

SAP Learning – Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Sourcing and Procurement

3. Learn Inventory Management as the bridge to EWM

Study stock types, movement types, valuation, batches, serial numbers and physical inventory

This is essential because EWM controls physical warehouse execution, while Inventory Management controls plant and storage-location stock and the related valuation postings

More details below:

SAP Learning – Inventory Management and Physical Inventory in SAP S/4HANA

4. Understand delivery and warehouse integration

Learn how purchase orders and sales orders generate inbound or outbound deliveries and how these become warehouse requests, warehouse tasks and warehouse orders

You should be able to follow the complete document flow from PO to inbound delivery, goods receipt, putaway and supplier invoice

More details below:

SAP Learning – Processes in SAP S/4HANA EWM

5. Learn Public Edition Warehouse Management

Focus on warehouse structure, inbound processing, putaway, picking, packing, replenishment, physical inventory, production integration and Fit-to-Standard implementation

Public Edition must be studied separately because its configuration and extensibility model differs from Private Edition

More details below:

SAP Learning – Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Warehouse Management

6. Move to full EWM configuration in Private Edition

Learn warehouse process types, putaway and stock-removal strategies, warehouse-order creation rules, storage control, waves, RF, replenishment, handling units and exception management

The recommended order is to learn EWM processes first and configuration second

More details below:

SAP Learning – Implementing Extended Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

SAP Learning – Applying Basic Customizing in SAP S/4HANA EWM

7. Finish with advanced physical logistics

Finally, study production supply, deconsolidation, cross-docking, yard processes, automation and MFS

More details below:

SAP Learning – Production Integration with SAP S/4HANA EWM

The key learning sequence is therefore:

S/4HANA foundations -> MM Procure-to-Pay -> Inventory Management -> delivery integration -> Public Edition Warehouse Management -> Private Edition EWM -> advanced warehouse processes

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Configuring Reason Codes and Action Codes for France B2B Supplier Invoices in SAP
entry.content:
@AdilHoussa please refer to Germany eInvoicing Mandate: Technical Pitfalls for... - SAP Community, Pitfall #5. 

When implementing the Peppol transmission channel, please be aware of the following information:

Verify what you are planning to use:

  1. Peppol Network - For B2B international invoices and B2G e-invoicing transmission through the Peppol standard network

  2. Approved Platform Mandate (Plateforme Agréée) - For direct submission to the French tax authorities through an approved dematerialization platform

Here's what you should know:

  • Peppol is supported by SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for B2G e-invoicing
  • The Approved Platform approach is the preferred channel depending on your company size and specific requirements
    • The French mandate timelines are:
      • 1 September 2026: Obligation for large and mid-sized enterprises
      • 1 September 2027: Obligation for small and micro companies

Further information:

  • Regulatory Change Manager

Peppol:

  • S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition / on premise Electronic Customer Invoices | SAP Help Portal
  • S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Electronic Customer Invoices | SAP Help Portal
  • ERP Electronic Customer Invoices | SAP Help Portal

Approved platform:

  • S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition / on premise Electronic Invoicing and Reporting via Approved Platform | SAP Help Portal
  • S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Electronic Invoicing and Electronic Reporting via Approved Platform | SAP Help Portal
  • ERP Electronic Invoicing and Reporting via Approved Platform | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: SAP's New API Policy Is Quietly Rewriting the BW Roadmap
entry.content:
SAP's New API Policy Is Quietly Rewriting the BW Roadmap

How API Policy v4/2026 and the June 2026 ODP-RFC enforcement patch are forcing BW and BW/4HANA customers toward Business Data Cloud and Datasphere

In late April 2026, SAP published an update to its API Policy on the SAP Help Portal with little advance warning. Since then, it has become one of the most consequential governance changes SAP has made to its integration landscape in years — and nowhere is the impact more immediate than in SAP BW and BW/4HANA, where a decades-old extraction mechanism is now being switched off at the technical level.

What the policy actually says

API Policy v4/2026 formalizes a distinction SAP has always implied but rarely enforced: only Published APIs — those listed on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub or explicitly named in product documentation — may be used by customers and partners. Internal, private, or “reserved-client” interfaces are off-limits except in narrow cases SAP explicitly authorizes, such as custom ABAP interfaces in private cloud or on-premise systems.

Two provisions have drawn the most attention:

Section 2.2.2 prohibits using SAP APIs to feed “(semi-)autonomous or generative AI systems that plan, select, or execute sequences of API calls” — in effect, third-party AI agents — unless the access runs through SAP-endorsed pathways such as Joule, SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC), or the Agent Gateway. It also restricts scraping and large-scale or systematic data extraction outside those same channels.

A general prohibition on non-published APIs, which covers RFC calls, BAPIs, and other interfaces that have quietly underpinned enterprise integrations — including most third-party data extraction tools — for two decades.

SAP CEO Christian Klein addressed the backlash on the company’s Q1 2026 investor call, saying customers won’t be charged to access their own data and that the intent is to protect SAP’s “domain know-how” — the semantic models and process logic embedded in its applications — rather than block customers from their own information. Critics, including the German-speaking user group DSAG, argue the distinction is harder to see in the policy text than in the reassurance, and that the practical effect is to funnel both AI and data-integration traffic toward SAP’s own platforms.

Why BW is ground zero

SAP BW and BW/4HANA extraction has, for years, run on ODP-RFC (Operational Data Provisioning over Remote Function Call) — the mechanism that gives third-party ETL tools, data lakes, and hyperscaler pipelines efficient delta/incremental access to BW InfoProviders and CDS-based extractors. It is exactly the kind of non-published, high-throughput interface the new policy targets.

SAP has already backed the policy with a technical enforcement date. Per SAP Notes 3255746 and 3439624, ODP-RFC is now designated for SAP-to-SAP communication only. A security patch, expected around June 9, 2026, adds a “subscriber type” check to every ODP call: if the calling application isn’t recognized as an SAP system, the call is blocked. Every major third-party extraction tool that reads BW or ECC/S4 data through ODP-RFC — from boutique SAP-data connectors to hyperscaler-native pipelines — sits on the wrong side of that line unless it’s rearchitected.

This lands on top of an already tight BW timeline. Mainstream maintenance for BW 7.5 ends in 2027 (extended maintenance runs to 2030 at extra cost), while BW/4HANA itself is maintained into 2040. Many organizations were already expected to decide, sometime in the next few years, whether to convert to BW/4HANA, move to SAP Datasphere, or exit BW altogether. The API policy doesn’t create that decision — but it removes the option of quietly deferring it by continuing to run existing extraction pipelines unchanged.

What changes on the BW roadmap

  1. The “do nothing” option disappears first. Nightly or continuous ODP-RFC extraction from BW into Snowflake, Databricks, Fabric, or a custom data lake — a pattern used across countless customer landscapes — stops working as of the June 2026 patch unless the tool vendor has rebuilt its connector on a compliant path. This is a hard, dated deadline, not a policy aspiration.
  2. SAP is steering customers toward Business Data Cloud and Datasphere. BDC is positioned as the sanctioned successor: a managed layer offering “zero-copy” data sharing (via BDC Connect, with a Snowflake connector marked delivered for AWS in Q2 2026) and an embedded, SAP-managed Databricks. For BW specifically, SAP is building a Data Product Generator — first available in 2026 — that converts classic BW InfoProviders and their logic into BDC/Datasphere data products, intended to preserve existing business logic while moving the platform underneath it.
  3. BW Bridge remains the migration path for BW modeling assets. For customers not ready for a full re-platform, SAP BW Bridge lets BW artifacts — data models, transformations, DTPs — be moved into a dedicated space inside Datasphere, where they become remote tables usable alongside native Datasphere objects. It’s a staging step toward Datasphere rather than a permanent architecture, and several BW capabilities (broadcasting, add-ons, classic authorizations, batch scheduling via typical job schedulers) aren’t yet at parity there.
  4. CDS views move up the priority list over classic extractors. Where a Data Product isn’t yet available for a given area, SAP’s own guidance now ranks released ABAP CDS views above ODP extractors and raw tables as the preferred, upgrade-stable integration point — reinforcing the “clean core” principle that already shapes S/4HANA extensibility.
  5. Direct HANA-layer access remains a narrower, license-gated option. Where BW runs on HANA, JDBC/ODBC access to the database layer can bypass ODP-RFC, but this depends on the specific HANA and BW licensing terms a customer holds, and isn’t a general-purpose substitute for governed extraction.

The strategic tension

SAP frames all of this as governance: standardizing chaotic, undocumented integration sprawl, protecting system performance from uncontrolled mass extraction, and safeguarding the semantic and process IP embedded in its applications. Consultancy voices, including SAPinsider’s research lead, have described it as SAP formalizing restrictions on patterns that were technically possible but never officially sanctioned — closing a gap that was already widely used in practice.

The counter-argument, voiced most clearly by DSAG, is that the policy narrows architectural choice at the same moment SAP is asking customers to modernize: if BDC, Datasphere, and Joule are simultaneously the recommended target platforms and the primary channels not subject to the new restrictions, customers migrating off BW have fewer independent alternatives than they did a year ago. For heavily regulated or multi-cloud organizations already committed to Databricks, Snowflake, or Fabric as their analytics home, this raises the practical cost of staying open — more custom CDS-view development, more reliance on SAP-mediated replication flows, and a tighter dependency on SAP’s own release cadence for parity features.

What this means for a BW roadmap today

For any organization still running BW or BW/4HANA, three things are now roadmap items rather than someday-considerations:

  • Inventory ODP-RFC dependencies immediately. Any nightly extraction job, CDC pipeline, or BI tool reading BW via ODP-RFC needs to be identified before the June 2026 enforcement date, not after something breaks in production.
  • Decide the target architecture deliberately, not by default. The realistic options are converting to BW/4HANA and adopting BDC-native data products, bridging into Datasphere via BW Bridge as a transitional layer, or rebuilding extraction on released CDS views and SAP-sanctioned replication flows if a non-SAP analytics platform remains the long-term home.
  • Treat the S/4HANA and BW/analytics roadmaps as one project. Because CDS views are now the preferred extraction interface and many are tied to S/4HANA’s data model, the timing of an S/4 conversion and a BW/Datasphere modernization can no longer be planned in isolation from each other.

The API policy didn’t set out to be a BW roadmap document. In practice, by cutting off the extraction mechanism BW has run on for twenty years, it has become one of the more decisive forcing functions the BW community has seen since SAP first announced the end-of-maintenance dates for on-premise BW.

 

Reference: 

- Note 3439624: discover and assess current ODP-RFC usage.

- Note 3748819: introduces the enforcement, and delivers the correction.

- Note 3731818: temporarily disables enforcement.

- Note 3255746: explain the intended-use policy.

 

Swati Shingade 

Swati Shingade | LinkedIn
