entry.title: From Preview to Production: Get Started with the SAP HANA Cloud's Agentic Data Exploration Tools
entry.content:
At SAP TechEd 2025, we shared a vision: what if exploring data felt less like archaeology and more like a conversation? We introduced two capabilities designed to make that real and the response told us we were onto something. Fast forward to today. That vision is now in production. SAP HANA Cloud's data exploration capabilities are generally available, and customers can start using them right now.

If you followed the TechEd announcement, you will remember these capabilities as the Discovery Agent and the Data Agent. As they matured from prototype to production, so did our thinking about what to call them.

They are now formally known as the Database Object Discovery Tool and the Data Retrieval Tool - together, the SAP HANA Cloud data exploration tools. The rename reflects a natural evolution: as these capabilities became production-grade, composable building blocks that any agent, application, or workflow can invoke, the "tool" framing became the more precise one. The underlying power is the same. 

Note: **Upcoming Agents. Subject to change as we evolve our roadmap

Why This Matters - For Everyone

Whether you are a developer building an AI-powered application, or a business stakeholder who simply wants answers from data, these tools close a gap that has existed for a long time.

For business users and decision makers:

  • You no longer need to know where data lives to find it. The Database Object Discovery Tool searches across tables, views, and relationships based on what you describe, or query about.
  • You no longer need SQL to get results. The Data Retrieval Tool translates a plain language request into a query and returns the answer, no manual lookup, no schema expertise required.
  • The experience feels conversational, even though sophisticated discovery and query generation is happening beneath the surface.

For developers and architects:

  • Production-grade interfaces: stored-procedure-based (AI_OBJECT_DISCOVERY and AI_DATA_RETRIEVAL) that integrate cleanly into any application or agent workflow.
  • Composable by design: wire them into an MCP server or call them directly from your own tooling & agents.
  • Context inherited automatically: the Custom Database Objects Knowledge Graph continues to serve as the semantic backbone, so your applications gain that contextual understanding without you having to build it.

Getting Started

There is no additional infrastructure to provision. If you have access to SAP HANA Cloud with the Natural Language Processing & Triple Store services enabled, and an SAP AI Core subscription, you have access to these tools today.

The pattern is straightforward:

  1. Discover - Describe what you are looking for in natural language. The Database Object Discovery Tool searches the HANA catalog's knowledge graph & RAG layer and returns the relevant tables, views, and columns.
  2. Retrieve - Pass that context to the Data Retrieval Tool along with your question. It generates and executes the appropriate query and returns results.

Try It Yourself -  Explore Data with SAP HANA Cloud Data Exploration Tools

The SAP Developer Tutorials has a step by step guide that walks you through setting up both tools and running your first natural-language query against SAP HANA Cloud. It covers the key features that make HANA Cloud well-suited for business AI, and gets you hands-on quickly.

SAP HANA Cloud Central Tooling

 

What's Next: Taking the Tools Further with MCP
 
These tools are built to be composable and one of the most powerful ways to use them is through the Model Context Protocol (MCP). In the next post in this series, we will walk through how to spin up a local MCP client that connects directly to the Database Object Discovery Tool and the Data Retrieval Tool, and what that unlocks for agentic AI workflows on top of SAP HANA Cloud.
If you have been curious about how SAP HANA Cloud fits into the broader agentic architecture picture at the protocol level that one is for you.
The Data Exploration Tools are more than a new capability, they mark SAP HANA Cloud's evolution toward an agentic database that not only manages data, but also enables users and AI agents to understand, explore, and leverage it intelligently. This is the direction SAP HANA Cloud is moving and there is more to come.

entry.title: Transform Your Fit-to-Standard Workshop Preparation with AI-Assisted Procedures
entry.content:
This blog post is part of a series highlighting AI capabilities in SAP Activate. For an overview of the complete series, visit the main blog post here: SAP Business AI in SAP Activate: Three Dimensions ... - SAP Community

What if AI could help your project team prepare Fit-to-Standard workshops faster, more consistently, and with greater confidence?

Now it can.

Why Fit-to-Standard preparation matters

Preparing for Fit-to-Standard workshops is one of the most important activities in an SAP implementation.

Before the workshops begin, the project team must:

  • Understand and validate the implementation scope.
  • Map the business processes and solutions to be demonstrated.
  • Identify the appropriate stakeholders.
  • Define clear roles and responsibilities.
  • Prepare a detailed workshop agenda.
  • Determine the required inputs and expected outcomes.

This preparation is essential—but it can also require significant time and coordination.

What if an AI-assisted procedure could help the project team perform these activities?

We have exciting news.

AI assistance is now embedded in SAP Activate

SAP Activate now includes AI-Assisted Procedures for selected tasks within the Application: Design and Configuration workstream of the SAP Cloud ERP Private – New Implementation roadmap.

These procedures combine SAP Activate best practices with generative AI to help project teams create comprehensive Fit-to-Standard workshop preparation and planning materials more efficiently.

Each AI-Assisted Procedure is:

  • Embedded directly in the task description in SAP Roadmap Viewer.
  • Connected to a task-specific prompt.
  • Supported by the SAP Activate Prompt Library.
  • Designed to fit naturally into the implementation workflow.

This means project teams can access AI guidance precisely where they need it—without leaving SAP Roadmap Viewer.

Let’s explore “Prepare for Fit-to-Standard Workshops”

Open the Prepare for Fit-to-Standard Workshops task in SAP Roadmap Viewer.

Inside the task, you will find an AI-Assisted Procedure that guides you through the workshop preparation process. Open the procedure and follow steps 1 through 9.

The procedure helps you organize the necessary project information and use the corresponding prompt to generate a structured Fit-to-Standard workshop preparation plan.

Two new accelerators to support the process

We have also created two accelerators to help project teams use the procedure and its prompt more effectively:

  1. Fit-to-Standard Workshop Notes — a structured template for capturing the information required to prepare and run the prompt.
  2. SAP Activate Prompt Library — a collection of prompts supporting multiple activities across the SAP Activate roadmaps, including the Prepare for Fit-to-Standard Workshops prompt.

The SAP Activate Prompt Library is available directly from the SAP Roadmap Viewer. Simply click the prompt referenced in the task to access it.

Each prompt entry provides consultants with the information they need to:

  • Understand the purpose of the prompt.
  • Identify the required inputs.
  • Execute the prompt correctly.
  • Review the expected output before getting started.

A structured prompt based on the COP Framework

The prompts follow the COP Prompt Framework, which is organized around:

  • Context — the project background and information the AI needs.
  • Objectives — the results the prompt should produce.
  • Preferences — the expected structure, level of detail, and output format.

This framework makes the prompts easier to understand, execute, and adapt to the specific needs of each project.

What does the prompt generate?

The Fit-to-Standard Workshop Preparation Plan prompt can generate:

  1. Scope Validation Checklist — a pre-workshop sign-off checklist for the System Integrator Lead and Customer Project Manager.
  2. Business Process Scoping Matrix — maps in-scope functional areas to SAP Best Practices scope item IDs, workshop dates, and business owners.
  3. Business Stakeholder Pre-Work — an actionable checklist of items due at least five business days before each workshop.
  4. System Readiness Checklist — covers sandbox provisioning, SAP Best Practices activation, master data, user access, and SAP Cloud ALM setup.
  5. Workshop Agenda Templates — includes a general full-day Fit-to-Standard agenda and tailored versions for functional areas such as Finance, Procure-to-Pay, Order-to-Cash, Manufacturing, Warehouse Management, and Asset Management.
  6. Fit-to-Standard Tracking Template — provides a detailed gap log, including one row for each delta requirement identified in the workshop notes.
  7. Roles and RACI Matrix — defines accountability across key customer, system integrator, architecture, consulting, and project management roles.
  8. Gap Capture Guidance for SAP Cloud ALM — provides a step-by-step process for recording gaps, completing mandatory attributes, assigning resolution categories, and maintaining traceability.
  9. Risks and Mitigation Strategies — creates a risk register covering workshop execution, scope, stakeholders, and technical readiness.
  10. Classification Decision Tree — supports consistent classification of requirements as Fit, Partial Fit, Gap, Out of Scope, or Deferred.

A representative example is also available in the Sample Output section of the prompt library.

What are the benefits?

AI-Assisted Procedures can help project teams:

  • Accelerate Fit-to-Standard workshop preparation.
  • Generate agendas based on the project scope.
  • Improve visibility into required resources, stakeholders, and inputs.
  • Reduce the time needed to validate workshop topics and schedules.
  • Establish a structured timetable for the topics to be discussed.
  • Apply SAP Activate guidance more consistently.
  • Start from a comprehensive, structured plan that the project team can review and refine.

AI supports the preparation—but the project team remains responsible for reviewing, validating, and adapting the output to the project context.

This is just the beginning

AI-Assisted Procedures bring SAP Activate guidance and generative AI together directly within the implementation workflow.

And this is only the beginning—more AI-assisted content is on the way.

Are you ready to use the Fit-to-Standard Workshop Preparation prompt in your next project?

Try it and share your experience in the comments. We would love to learn how you used the prompt, what value it brought to your project, and which SAP Activate task you would like to see supported next.

entry.title: Re: Guidance on SuccessFactors LMS Certification – New Ex...
entry.content:
Please find details below related to the SAP SuccessFactors Learning certification exam: 

Link to the exam (1H 2026 release): SAP Certified - SAP SuccessFactors Learning

Exam type: This is an open-book, scenario-based exam and you have 2 options to complete it:

  • AI-guided option (recommended):
    Complete your scenario-based assessment through a virtual interview with an AI-guided tool. You will be asked real-world questions during a simulated interview and your responses will be recorded to provide you with personalized feedback. The cut score is 60% and you have up to 2 hours to complete the exam using the AI-guided tool. You will receive your exam result at the end of the assessment.
  • Human reviewer:
    Run through a scenario-assessment by recording a video. You will be provided with speaking points and instructions on how to record and upload the video that will be reviewed by an SAP expert. Once submitted, your video will be validated before results are released. With this certification method, it might take up to 20 business days for you to receive your exam result.

Link to FAQs: For more information on scenario-based exams, please access this link: SAP Certification practical exams - Scenario-based assessment FAQs. This also includes a brief video on the AI-guided (recommended) option.

Link to the Learning Journey to help you prepare for the exam: Configuring SAP SuccessFactors Learning.

entry.title: Re: Overtimes to more time accounts
entry.content:
Hi Jan, 

You can store the results in a Collector or better Period Result Container (based on daily Container). Container has the advantage that it is created only if a result exists. 

The result, hours, you can write via Integration Center to the time account details, the externalCode of the time account can be filtered via Container-User-TimeAccount(filtered by time account type). For the booking you need an externalCode which is constant, don't use the externalCode of the container this changes when there is a recalculation. You can use a combination if USERID_TTG_PERIOENDDATE (all fields within the Time Container)... This code will always be constant for the period. Meaning if there is later a recalculation the same booij g gets updated. IC scheduling you have to play, I would only include containers if the period end date is reached or is backdated (later updates).

The standard TOIL account can be linked via TTG as a standard connection same for WTA

Maybe this is a starting point for you.

Best regards 

Patrik

entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud Q2 ‘26 Release Highlights
entry.content:
SAP Commerce Cloud Q2 2026 Release Highlights

The Q2 2026 release of SAP Commerce Cloud continues our commitment to delivering intelligent, composable, and scalable commerce experiences. This release introduces meaningful enhancements across storefront architecture, integrations, payments, and user experience, enabling organizations to simplify operations while accelerating time to value.

At the same time, unveiled a forward-looking set of AI-driven innovations and ecosystem partnerships that define the future direction of SAP Commerce Cloud at this year’s Sapphire. These capabilities are planned to be available later this year and provide a clear vision of how commerce will evolve toward more connected, intelligent, and autonomous experiences.

Together, the delivered innovations and upcoming capabilities demonstrate SAP’s continued investment in helping businesses innovate faster, adapt to change, and deliver exceptional customer journeys at scale.

AI-Driven Innovation and Ecosystem Expansion (SAPPHIRE Announcements)

SAP unveiled a new wave of AI-driven innovations and strategic partnerships at SAPPHIRE, offering a strong outlook on the future of SAP Commerce Cloud. These announcements outline SAP’s vision of seamlessly connecting discovery, conversion, and fulfillment through intelligent, AI-powered commerce experiences.

Highlights include AI Assistants for Commerce and expanded collaborations with Google and Vercel. These innovations are designed to embed intelligence across workflows, enable highly personalized customer interactions, and provide modern frontend flexibility. While not part of this release, they represent a significant step forward toward AI-first and ecosystem-driven commerce.

Decoupled Storefront Hosting Service

SAP Commerce Cloud introduces a new decoupled Storefront Hosting Service that separates frontend and backend builds, enabling faster deployment cycles and improved scalability. By isolating storefront delivery from core commerce operations, organizations gain greater flexibility in how they manage and evolve their digital experiences.

With capabilities such as server-side rendering, built-in caching, optional CDN integration, and significantly faster build times, merchants can deliver consistently high-performance storefronts. This results in improved customer experiences while reducing development bottlenecks and accelerating innovation cycles.

Composable AI Integration with MCP

Model Context Protocol (MCP) introduces a standardized approach for connecting AI agents and conversational interfaces with live commerce systems. This enables real-time interaction between AI tools and core commerce data, allowing assistants to provide accurate and up-to-date responses.

With MCP, conversational AI assistants can go beyond static responses by directly accessing live product catalogs, pricing, promotions, and inventory data, as well as executing transactional operations such as cart management and checkout. This transforms AI interactions into fully operational commerce experiences, unlocking new engagement 

With MCP, conversational AI assistants can go beyond static responses by directly accessing live product catalogs, pricing, promotions, and inventory data, as well as executing transactional operations such as cart management and checkout. This transforms AI interactions into fully operational commerce experiences, unlocking new engagement models and driving more intuitive customer journeys.

 

Service Provider Interfaces and Resilience

Service Provider Interfaces (SPI) extend SAP Commerce Cloud’s composability by enabling external services to plug into core commerce processes such as pricing, delivery, and tax calculation. This allows organizations to adopt best-in-class solutions without disrupting existing systems.

Combined with built-in resilience capabilities such as retries, circuit breakers, and timeout controls, SPI ensures stability in distributed architectures. Businesses benefit from both flexibility and reliability, allowing them to scale and innovate confidently in complex system landscapes.

OMS Flow Connector

The OMS Flow Connector simplifies integration across distributed order management environments by enabling seamless data flow between SAP Order Management Services, SAP solutions, and third-party systems. This eliminates the need for complex custom integrations and accelerates connectivity across the enterprise.

Business users can configure workflows and orchestrate integrations with minimal IT involvement, empowering teams to quickly adapt to changing business needs. This leads to faster process innovation, improved agility, and more efficient order orchestration across channels and fulfillment networks.

 

Open Payment Framework Enhancements

Enhancements to the Open Payment Framework introduce advanced split payment capabilities to support modern commerce use cases. Merchants can combine multiple payment methods within a single transaction, including gift cards and digital payments, while also supporting marketplace scenarios with multiple sellers.

These capabilities simplify complex payment orchestration and reduce friction at checkout. By enabling more flexible payment experiences, businesses can increase conversion rates while supporting evolving business models such as marketplaces and omnichannel commerce.

 

Wishlist Enhancements

Wishlist functionality has been improved to provide a more seamless customer experience across anonymous and authenticated journeys. Wishlists created before login can now be automatically merged with a user’s account, ensuring continuity of product interest and intent.

This enhancement reduces friction in the shopping journey and allows customers to effortlessly continue their experience across sessions and devices. As a result, businesses benefit from stronger engagement, better personalization opportunities, and improved conversion outcomes.

The Q2 2026 release reinforces SAP Commerce Cloud as a future-ready platform that combines performance, composability, and innovation. By delivering both immediate value and a strong vision for the future, SAP continues to help organizations build resilient, scalable, and differentiated commerce experiences.

 

 

For more details on the above release features, check out the following resources: 

  • SAP Commerce Cloud Release Navigator single access point to release content 
  • Replay of the Q2 2026 SAP Commerce Cloud Release Recap webcast 
  • Register for the Q2 2026 Tech-Focused Update webcast
  • Sign up for the monthly SAP Commerce newsletter
  • Read the SAP Customer Experience Q2 2026 blog

SAP Help Portal 

  • SAP Commerce Cloud What’s New 

2026 Holiday Season Readiness Program - Proactively Preparing for Peak with Confidence

  • Sign up for the Holiday Season Readiness Program

entry.title: Re: Improving SAP HANA DB Performance: Two Common CDS View Pitfalls and How to Fix Them
entry.content:
Hi,

very detailed and well written. Thanks.

If have 2 things to add:

  1. HEX engine is not the best choice by default. It is in most cases but as you can see in this example it has missing optimiziations. We are on 2.00.79 and are using a CDS for a query not containing text associations. The query itself is fine. HANA automatically chose HEX engine after an HANA update. The execution time went from 5-20 ms to 2.5 s. SAP requested us to exclude HEX in this CDS by a hint. Fixes coming with SPS8.
    Long story short: As @cohen_almog wrote, first analyze if you really have a problem! Do not throw hints at every CDS hoping it would do magic. HEX is better in most cases but there are still edge cases.
  2. Doesn't FOR ALL ENTRIES with empty local table return ALL data from the database? Every static code check has those checks.

entry.title: Extracting CO planning data in S/4HANA for Datasphere/SAC
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

In our current project we completed a migration to SAP S/4HANA and we are now starting to use SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud for reporting (we're new with SAP reporting).

Before the migration, one of our reporting model relied on extractions from tables COSP and COSS. From what I understand, in S/4HANA the actuals are now stored in ACDOCA (type 4 and 11), and based on the available Datasphere business content I found the CDS view that allows me to extract actual cost data from ACDOCA.

However, I am unsure how to handle the planning data that previously came from COSP and COSS. Are these planning records now stored in ACDOCP in S/4HANA? Or do I need to design and develop a custom CDS view or another extraction process to retrieve planning data for COSP and COSS reporting?

Any guidance or best practices for handling COSP/COSS planning data in S/4HANA (especially when integrating with Datasphere) would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks and regards.

entry.title: Re: LMS Assignment Profile based on completion status ID/...
entry.content:
Hi @stephanie5 

Currently, the assignment profile's rule 'Item Completion' only works for completions were credits were given (more commonly, the setting 'Credit given' is enabled for successful completion statuses and disabled for failures, but that's up to each customer to define).

So a separate AP with a rule considering failed completion statuses is not possible:

  • The rule 'Item Completion' considers the item against the completion when a credit was given to it, regardless of the completion status ID;
  • Even if both successful and failed statues have the setting 'Credit given' enabled, the system will consider both completions equally: the item was completed with credit.

2622074 - Assignment Profile is Not Recognizing "Item Completion" Rule in SuccessFactors Learning - SAP for Me

2185985 - How to create failure statuses in Learning? - SAP for Me

https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-learning/managing-sap-successfactors-learning-for-administrators/adding-course-completion-status?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

---------------------

There have been some similar enhancements requests on this topic, and every customer is welcomed to vote for any open requests and/or create additional ones:

  • https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/222103 
  • https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/341481 
  • https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/262748 

For more details on enhancement requests, please check the KBA 2090228 - How to submit enhancement ideas for SAP SuccessFactors Products.

Best regards,
